page: Amazon Web Services m6in.metal is a general-purpose bare metal server featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. Operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this dedicated instance includes 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor features 54 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a high baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m6in.metal demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, scoring 124977.74 in stress-ng multi-core tests and 87838.82 in Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 6701.46 GB/sec and large block uncached speeds at 339.05 GB/sec. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are also in the top decile, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU encryption score of 113494.66 and strong ray tracing and image processing results. Single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall into the average tier, indicating that the server's strength lies in highly parallelized workloads.

Given its bare metal nature and high resource density, the m6in.metal offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise workloads requiring direct hardware access without virtualization overhead. The combination of 200 Gbps network bandwidth and high-capacity DDR4 memory makes it highly suitable for network-intensive applications, large-scale database operations, and high-throughput Redis deployments, where it achieves 112.88 million operations per second. It is also well-suited for multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and moderate LLM prompt processing workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.metal is a general-purpose bare metal server powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. It features 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 54 MB L3 cache. The server lacks local storage and GPUs, but delivers a high baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. In benchmarks, the instance excels in multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic execution, placing in the top tier for stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests. Conversely, single-core performance remains average. This bare metal configuration is highly efficient for parallelized enterprise workloads, including high-throughput Redis deployments, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.metal is a bare metal server with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.metal is a bare metal server featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and a high-speed 200 Gbps network interface.
tagline: A high-bandwidth bare metal instance delivering 128 dedicated vCPUs and top-tier multi-core performance for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
512.0 GB of DDR4 memory with top-tier read bandwidth up to 6701.46 GB/sec
High-speed 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for intensive data transfer
Excellent multi-core performance and cryptographic acceleration capabilities
No local storage or GPU, optimized for network-attached storage
Best suited for Redis deployments, database operations, and web serving
